name: Tate McRae
description:
'''Tate Rosner McRae''' (born July 1, 2003) is a Canadian singer, songwriter, and dancer. She first gained prominence as a contestant on the American reality television series ''[[So You Think You Can Dance (American TV series)|So You Think You Can Dance]]'' in 2016. She signed with [[RCA Records]] and gained early recognition for her [[extended play]]s (EPs) ''[[All the Things I Never Said (Tate McRae EP)|All the Things I Never Said]]'' (2020) and ''[[Too Young to Be Sad]]'' (2021); the latter became the most streamed female EP of 2021 on [[Spotify]] and was preceded by the single "[[You Broke Me First]]", her first US [[Billboard Hot 100|''Billboard'' Hot 100]] entry.

In 2022, McRae released her debut studio album, ''[[I Used to Think I Could Fly]]'', which peaked at number three on the [[Canadian music charts]] and was supported by the single "[[She's All I Wanna Be]]". Her second studio album, ''[[Think Later]]'' (2023), explored a more [[pop music|pop]]-oriented sound and produced the [[List of music recording certifications|multi-platinum]] single "[[Greedy (Tate McRae song)|Greedy]]", which became one of the bestselling songs of 2024 globally. In 2025, McRae released her third studio album, ''[[So Close to What]]'', which topped the US [[Billboard 200|''Billboard'' 200]] and yielded the global top-ten hits "[[Sports Car (song)|Sports Car]]", "[[Revolving Door (Tate McRae song)|Revolving Door]]", and "[[Tit for Tat (Tate McRae song)|Tit for Tat]]". That same year, she scored her first US number-one single as a featured artist on [[Morgan Wallen]]'s "[[What I Want (Morgan Wallen song)|What I Want]]" and earned her first [[Grammy Award for Best Dance Pop Recording|Grammy Award]] nomination for the [[F1 the Album|soundtrack]] single "[[Just Keep Watching]]".
